title: Empirical stint-hazard baseline blended with residual GBDT
summary: Discrete-time survival references fit this label structure exactly: probability of event at lap t given survival until t.
rationale: Discrete-time survival references fit this label structure exactly: probability of event at lap t given survival until t. Instead of a fully generic classifier, build a strong empirical baseline hazard from historical stint outcomes conditioned on race regime, then let boosting learn residual deviations.
implementation_hint: From training data, construct past-only hazard tables for `P(pit next lap | compound, stint, tyre-life bucket, laps remaining bucket, wet/slick, race or race family)` with smoothing. Feed the table lookup, cumulative survival-to-now, and baseline-minus-observed pace residuals into LightGBM/CatBoost as first-class features.
expected_effect: Often gives a stronger inductive bias than raw tyre thresholds and stabilizes predictions for rare regimes like wets or long late stints.
risk: Over-conditioning can sparsify tables; must smooth aggressively and compute everything fold-locally.
